scope: SWE-agent paper-era protocol and missing-artifact audit

sweagent.paper_period_definition: pull request number <= 621, covering public PRs through June 2024

protocol_components.evidence: Frozen default config contains WINDOW=100, Last5Observations, summarized search, linting editor, and one demonstration.
Full-history configuration is present at the frozen revision; paper-run predictions and trajectories are absent.
Cursor-viewer configurations contain WINDOW=200 despite window100 in their filenames; run-to-table mapping and raw sweep outputs are absent.
The configuration schema permits an empty demonstrations list, but no exact paper-run config or raw outputs were found.
The summarized search command can be removed from a config, but no exact paper-run config or outputs were found.
The editor command can be removed from a config, but no exact paper-run config or outputs were found.
WINDOW is configurable, but no WINDOW=30 file, commit, PR head, trajectory, or result was found.
The paper describes the behavior, but no exact command implementation or config was found.
Only linting editor command files occur in the paper-era public history and PR heads.
No next/prev iterative-search implementation occurs in the paper-era public history or PR heads.
The paper identifies InterCode-Bash as the conceptual basis, but no exact Shell-only agent configuration, prompt, trajectories, or predictions were released.

negative_findings: No official dev-37 instance ID manifest was found.
No five-sample hyperparameter sweep trajectories, predictions, seeds, or evaluation logs were found.
No six-run pass@k prediction sets or per-instance resolution matrix were found.
No 248 generated failure labels, 15 validation IDs, or hand labels were found.
No Shell-only or Shell-only-without-demonstration raw run was found.
No raw outputs for the eight ACI single-factor ablations were found.
No additional paper-era ablation or pass@k run prefix appears in the public S3 bucket listing.
conclusion: The P1 asset audit is complete. Missing official assets are explicit blockers for exact artifact replay; paper aggregates remain reproducible but cannot substitute for raw-run recomputation.
